checklist_item.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity for tb-010: Is the compute_fork_digest hashing scheme for blob parameters implemented identically across all client implementations?

reachability.notes: Any deviation in byte encoding (e.g., uint64 vs uint256, big-endian vs little-endian) or concatenation order (epoch+max_blobs vs max_blobs+epoch) would produce different hashes, causing network partitions.

checklist_item.test_procedure: 1. Identify all client implementations of compute_fork_digest for blob parameters. 2. Verify the uint_to_bytes encoding specification: check byte width (uint64 vs uint256), endianness (big-endian vs little-endian), and padding behavior. 3. Verify concatenation order: confirm all clients compute hash(uint_to_bytes(epoch) + uint_to_bytes(max_blobs_per_block)) in exactly that order. 4. Verify the hash function used is identical across all implementations. 5. Create test vectors with known blob_parameters.epoch and blob_parameters.max_blobs_per_block values. 6. Execute compute_fork_digest on each client implementation with identical inputs. 7. Compare hash outputs across all clients - any difference indicates a critical consensus bug that would cause network partitions.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W1B10-60, Trust Boundary: tb-010, Assumption: asm-010, Threat: stride-008. This is a CRITICAL boundary property - encoding disagreements would cause network partitions isolating non-conforming clients.

checklist_item.title: Verify Consensus Layer Implementation: Does actor-consensus-layer correctly implement the canonical blob parameter hashing invariant?

reachability.notes: Any deviation in byte encoding (e.g., uint64 vs uint256, big-endian vs little-endian) or concatenation order (epoch+max_blobs vs max_blobs+epoch) would produce different hashes, causing network partitions.

checklist_item.test_procedure: 1. Locate the consensus layer code responsible for fork digest computation (SG-003). 2. Review the implementation of uint_to_bytes encoding - verify it matches the specification exactly. 3. Check for any platform-specific or language-specific behaviors that could affect byte representation. 4. Verify that the hash function used matches the canonical specification (likely SHA256 or similar). 5. Check for any edge cases: zero values, maximum values, boundary conditions for epoch and max_blobs_per_block. 6. Review any serialization libraries used - ensure they produce consistent output. 7. Verify that no implicit type conversions could alter the byte representation.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W1B10-60, Supporting node check for actor-consensus-layer. Even a single byte difference in hash input produces completely different digest output.

checklist_item.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Is keccak256 hash of RLP-encoded trie deterministic regardless of insertion order?
checklist_item.test_procedure: 1. Identify all entry points for transaction submission that affect trie state. 2. Craft two valid transaction sequences that produce identical final state but differ in ordering. 3. Execute both sequences and compare resulting state roots. 4. Verify that root(T1) == root(T2) for identical key-value mappings. 5. Test with block producers controlling transaction ordering.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-0001, Trust Boundaries: tb-001, tb-007. Violation would cause consensus splits between nodes processing same transactions in different orders.

checklist_item.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Is the 32-byte RLP encoding threshold consistently enforced in encode_internal_node?
checklist_item.test_procedure: 1. Craft state entries that produce trie nodes with RLP encodings at exactly 32 bytes. 2. Test boundary condition: verify len(rlp.encode(...)) < 32 returns unencoded form. 3. Verify len(rlp.encode(...)) >= 32 returns keccak256 hash. 4. Compare handling across different client implementations. 5. Check for off-by-one errors in comparison (< vs <=).
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-0003, Trust Boundaries: tb-002, tb-004, tb-008. 32-byte boundary is a well-known edge case in MPT implementations.

checklist_item.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Is storage_root validated as non-None for all Account nodes in encode_node?
checklist_item.test_procedure: 1. Create contract creation transaction with both code and initial storage. 2. Test race conditions between storage root computation and account encoding. 3. Verify assertion fires if storage_root is None. 4. Check what happens if None storage_root is silently used (incorrect state root computation). 5. Test Fusaka-specific storage layout changes.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-0004, Trust Boundary: tb-006. DoS if assertion fires; consensus failure if None is silently used.

checklist_item.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Are all Fusaka/Osaka-specific types explicitly handled in encode_node before fallback?
checklist_item.test_procedure: 1. Review encode_node isinstance checks for exhaustiveness. 2. Identify all new Fusaka transaction and receipt types. 3. Verify each Fusaka type is handled before previous_trie.encode_node fallback. 4. Test with new EIP-7702 set-code transactions if applicable. 5. Compare encoding outputs between Fusaka and previous fork encoders.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-0005, Trust Boundary: tb-005. Conditional scope - requires demonstrating Fusaka type reaching fallback path.

checklist_item.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Does copy_trie produce true isolation between original and copy?
checklist_item.test_procedure: 1. Create trie copy and verify _data modifications to copy do not affect original. 2. Test with block producers controlling transaction ordering during re-org. 3. Check if shallow copy shares mutable state with original. 4. Verify secured and default fields are preserved. 5. Test concurrent access to original and copy during speculative execution.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-0006, Trust Boundary: tb-007. Non-deterministic state roots possible during re-org processing.

checklist_item.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Does trie_set properly delete keys when value equals default?
checklist_item.test_procedure: 1. Submit transaction that sets storage value to default (e.g., zero). 2. Verify key is removed from trie._data after operation. 3. Check for phantom entries remaining in trie. 4. Compare state root before and after default-value set. 5. Test with various EVM operations (SSTORE with zero, SELFDESTRUCT).
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-0007, Trust Boundary: tb-001. Phantom entries would alter state root.

checklist_item.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Is trie_set/trie_get roundtrip guaranteed for non-default values?
checklist_item.test_procedure: 1. Craft values that might compare equal to trie.default through type confusion. 2. Test trie_set with various value types (Account, Bytes, U256). 3. Verify trie_get returns exact value set. 4. Check for unintended deletion of non-default values. 5. Test edge cases: empty bytes vs None, zero U256 vs default.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-0008, Trust Boundary: tb-001. Unauthorized fund loss if balances silently deleted.

checklist_item.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Is nibble_list_to_compact encoding is_leaf flag and parity correct?
checklist_item.test_procedure: 1. Generate nibble lists of various lengths (odd and even). 2. Verify first_byte_high_nibble == (2 * is_leaf) + (len(x) % 2). 3. Craft Merkle proofs with nodes at compact encoding boundary. 4. Test proof verification to ensure leaf/extension nodes distinguished. 5. Attempt to forge proofs by confusing leaf/extension via compact encoding.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-0012, Trust Boundary: tb-008. Merkle proof forgery possible if leaf/extension confused.

checklist_item.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Are bytes_to_nibble_list and nibble_list_to_compact mutual inverses?
checklist_item.test_procedure: 1. Test roundtrip: decode(compact(nibbles(bytes))) == bytes. 2. Craft proof paths with various byte sequences. 3. Check compact header stripping produces original data. 4. Test edge cases: empty paths, single-byte, 32-byte keys. 5. Attempt proof path manipulation exploiting encode/decode asymmetry.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-0015, Trust Boundaries: tb-004, tb-008. Proof forgery possible if not exact inverses.

checklist_item.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Does encode_node produce non-empty output for all valid values?
checklist_item.test_procedure: 1. Identify all Fusaka-specific types in encode_node. 2. Test each type to ensure non-empty encoding (not b''). 3. Craft transactions triggering each encoding path. 4. Verify assertion in _prepare_trie catches empty encodings. 5. Test boundary conditions between valid and invalid state values.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-0016, Trust Boundary: tb-001. All nodes crash if valid block triggers empty encoding.

checklist_item.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Is keccak256 key hashing enforced for secured tries?
checklist_item.test_procedure: 1. Verify trie.secured == True for state trie. 2. Confirm _prepare_trie hashes all keys with keccak256. 3. Check for scenarios where secured flag could be False. 4. Test if attacker can control trie structure via crafted addresses. 5. Attempt DoS through pathological trie shapes with unhashed keys.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-0017, Trust Boundary: tb-001. DoS possible if key hashing bypassed.

checklist_item.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Is get_storage_root callback correctly invoked with proper Address?
checklist_item.test_procedure: 1. Verify get_storage_root callback is non-None for Account values. 2. Confirm correct Address derived from preimage. 3. Test with Fusaka-specific storage layout changes. 4. Verify storage root computation for all account types. 5. Check callback behavior during concurrent account modifications.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-0018, Trust Boundary: tb-006. Most impactful single point of failure - affects all accounts.

checklist_item.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Does empty trie produce correct EMPTY_TRIE_ROOT?
checklist_item.test_procedure: 1. Create empty trie and verify root() == EMPTY_TRIE_ROOT. 2. Confirm EMPTY_TRIE_ROOT == keccak256(rlp.encode(b'')). 3. Test scenario where all state entries are deleted. 4. Verify consistency with expected constant value. 5. Check edge case: trie becoming empty through SELFDESTRUCT.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-0019, Trust Boundary: tb-003. Consensus failure if empty trie produces wrong root.

checklist_item.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Is root() output always exactly 32 bytes?
checklist_item.test_procedure: 1. Test tries with very few entries producing small root nodes. 2. Verify root() always returns 32-byte output. 3. Check that small roots are keccak256 hashed. 4. Craft blocks leaving exact number of entries to trigger edge case. 5. Compare root size handling across client implementations.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-0020, Trust Boundaries: tb-002, tb-003. Non-32-byte root causes validation failures.

checklist_item.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Does patricialize correctly handle common prefix extension nodes?
checklist_item.test_procedure: 1. Create transactions producing keys with specific common prefixes. 2. Verify ExtensionNode returned for shared prefix > 0. 3. Test recursive patricialize at level + prefix_length. 4. Check cross-client consistency in prefix computation. 5. Test edge cases: very long prefixes, single-nibble prefixes.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-0023, Trust Boundary: tb-002. Incorrect extension nodes produce wrong state roots.

checklist_item.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Are Account/Receipt/Uint rejected at internal branch value positions?
checklist_item.test_procedure: 1. Craft transactions mapping Account/Receipt/Uint to keys matching branch level. 2. Verify AssertionError raised for these types at branch positions. 3. Test with len(key) == level condition. 4. Check all Fusaka-specific types for this constraint. 5. Attempt to bypass through key collision at branch levels.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-0025, Trust Boundaries: tb-001, tb-002. Network halt if all clients crash.

checklist_item.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Does patricialize guarantee termination through increasing level?
checklist_item.test_procedure: 1. Analyze patricialize recursion invariant: level' > level for all recursive calls. 2. Craft input attempting to cause non-increasing level. 3. Test with pathological key distributions. 4. Verify no infinite recursion possible. 5. Check stack overflow behavior with deep recursion.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-0026, Trust Boundaries: tb-001, tb-002. Stack overflow crashes node during block processing.

checklist_item.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Is keccak256 collision resistance assumed correctly?
checklist_item.test_procedure: 1. Verify keccak256 implementation correctness against test vectors. 2. Confirm no known collision vulnerabilities. 3. Check for weak hash usage in any code paths. 4. Verify collision probability < 2^-128. 5. Document cryptographic assumption for audit trail.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-0027, Trust Boundaries: tb-001, tb-002, tb-004, tb-008. Foundation of state verification mechanism.

checklist_item.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Is RLP encoding canonical and deterministic?
checklist_item.test_procedure: 1. Test rlp.encode produces identical output for identical inputs. 2. Verify no two distinct inputs produce same RLP encoding. 3. Check for non-canonical encoding edge cases (leading zeros, length prefixes). 4. Compare RLP output across client implementations. 5. Test with complex nested structures in trie nodes.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-0028, Trust Boundaries: tb-002, tb-004. Non-canonical encoding breaks trie node hash uniqueness.

checklist_item.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Are nibble values validated in range [0, 15]?
checklist_item.test_procedure: 1. Craft compact encodings that decode to out-of-range nibbles. 2. Verify nibble_list_to_compact rejects or handles values > 15. 3. Test Merkle proof verification with invalid nibbles. 4. Check for overlapping paths caused by out-of-range nibbles. 5. Attempt proof forgery through nibble overflow.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-0029, Trust Boundaries: tb-002, tb-004, tb-008. Ambiguous trie paths enable proof forgery.

checklist_item.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Is P2P block state root verification enforced?
checklist_item.test_procedure: 1. Receive block from malicious peer with crafted transactions. 2. Execute transactions and compute state root. 3. Verify block header state root matches computed root. 4. Test block rejection when state root mismatches. 5. Check for bypass through edge cases in trie construction.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-0030, Trust Boundary: tb-002. Primary defense against forged blocks.

checklist_item.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Is patricialize complexity bounded by O(N * K)?
checklist_item.test_procedure: 1. Create transactions with many accounts having long common prefixes. 2. Measure patricialize computation time. 3. Compare computation cost to EVM gas paid. 4. Test for worse-than-polynomial complexity. 5. Verify per-block gas limits provide adequate protection.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-0031, Trust Boundary: tb-001. Gas/computation asymmetry enables DoS.

checklist_item.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Are state sync trie nodes verified against known root?
checklist_item.test_procedure: 1. Receive trie nodes from sync provider. 2. Verify keccak256(rlp.encode(node)) matches parent reference. 3. Test 32-byte inline/hash boundary handling. 4. Craft nodes exploiting boundary inconsistencies across clients. 5. Verify rejection of nodes failing verification.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-0032, Trust Boundary: tb-004. Invalid nodes corrupt local state.
